FERS RETIREMENT PLANNING UTAH

Utah Federal Employees: Maximize Your FERS Benefits

Working at Hill AFB, IRS Ogden, or VA Salt Lake City? Your FERS retirement has moving parts that most advisors don't understand. We specialize in federal retirement planning and help you optimize all three components of your federal pension.

GET YOUR FERS ANALYSIS →

Utah federal employees who optimize their FERS benefits can increase their retirement income by $2,000+ per month and save over $100,000 in unnecessary taxes.

Avg. Monthly Income Increase $2,100
Utah Federal Employees Served 200+
TSP Tax Savings $125K

THE PROBLEM

Utah Federal Workers Face These Challenges

Your TSP is invested like it's 1995

Most federal employees have their entire TSP in the G Fund earning 2-3%. Meanwhile, inflation eats away your purchasing power and you're missing decades of potential growth.

You're confused about your pension calculation

What counts as your "high-3"? How do unused sick days affect your pension? Is it better to retire at your MRA or wait until 62? These decisions affect your income for life.

Nobody explains the supplement

The special retirement supplement can bridge the gap until Social Security — but it has earning limits and complex rules. Most advisors don't even know it exists.

FEGLI costs are killing you after 65

Your Federal Employee Group Life Insurance might cost $2,000+ per month after age 65. Most retirees don't realize they can drop it and buy cheaper coverage elsewhere.

THE SOLUTION

Complete FERS Optimization for Utah Federal Employees

We Understand Federal Benefits

Capital Wealth specializes in FERS retirement planning. We understand the three-legged stool: your pension, Social Security, and TSP. More importantly, we know how to coordinate all three with Utah's tax landscape.

We work with federal employees throughout Utah — from Hill Air Force Base in Northern Utah to the IRS Ogden Service Center, VA Salt Lake City, and DoD installations statewide. We understand shift work, locality pay, and the unique challenges of federal employment.

20+ Years Helping Utah federal employees maximize FERS benefits

Utah Federal Locations We Serve:

  • Hill Air Force Base (Ogden) — Air Force Materiel Command
  • IRS Ogden Service Center — Tax processing operations
  • VA Salt Lake City Medical Center — Veterans healthcare
  • Social Security Administration offices statewide
  • USDA Forest Service — Utah national forests
  • National Park Service — Utah's "Big Five" parks
  • Bureau of Land Management Utah offices
  • All other federal agencies in Utah

FERS Planning Process

Comprehensive federal retirement planning in four steps.

START YOUR ANALYSIS
Step 1) Benefits Audit ▾

Complete review of your SF-50s, TSP statements, pension estimates, and FEGLI elections. We identify optimization opportunities you might be missing.

Step 2) Retirement Timing Analysis ▾

Calculate optimal retirement timing considering your MRA, penalty reductions, special retirement supplement, and Utah tax implications.

Step 3) TSP Optimization ▾

Optimize TSP allocation, maximize agency matching, plan withdrawal strategies, and coordinate traditional vs. Roth TSP contributions.

Step 4) Implementation & Monitoring ▾

Execute benefit changes, coordinate with federal HR systems, and provide ongoing monitoring as you approach retirement eligibility.

COMMON QUESTIONS

Utah FERS Planning FAQ

Answers specific to Utah federal employees and FERS benefits.
